title: Mood Manipulation and PST, Experiment 2
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 50 healthy control participants performing a reinforcement learning task under two mood conditions: sad mood manipulation (n=25) and happy mood manipulation (n=25). The task includes training and testing phases with mood induction occurring before each training block. Data were collected between 2019-2021 at the Cognitive Rhythms and Computation Lab at the University of New Mexico. PST refers to the probabilistic selection task used in this experiment.
methods_description: Reinforcement learning task with mood manipulation (sad vs. happy conditions) administered before training blocks. Task adapted from Daw et al. (2006, Science 312:1674-1677, DOI: 10.1126/science.1102941). Mood induction occurred prior to each training block, with separate training and testing sections. Task implemented in MATLAB.
